---
title: "pan_virus" 
---



```{r}
setwd("~/Desktop/Pan_virus_AI_Surrey_2026/")

library(readxl)
#BiocManager::install("limma")
library(limma)
library(ggplot2)
library(matrixStats)
library(stringr) 
library(reshape2)

# ===============================
# 2. Load data
# ===============================
df <- read_excel("pan_virus_surrey.xlsx")
gene_names <- df[[1]]   # first column explicitly
df <- df[, -1] # Remove gene column
# Convert to numeric safely
df[] <- lapply(df, function(x) as.numeric(as.character(x)))
E <- as.matrix(df) # Convert to matrix
# reattach gene names correctly
rownames(E) <- gene_names
mode(E) <- "numeric"

# 3. Remove unwanted columns
#remove_cols <- c( "A549_Mock_33C_1", "A549_Mock_33C_2", "A549_Mock_33C_3")
remove_cols <- c(  )
keep_cols <- !(colnames(E) %in% remove_cols)
E <- E[, keep_cols]

# ===============================
# 4. Build metadata AFTER filtering
# ===============================
samples <- colnames(E)

host <- ifelse(grepl("^A549", samples), "human",
         ifelse(grepl("^PK-15", samples), "pig",
         ifelse(grepl("^MDBK", samples), "cow", NA)))

virus <- str_split(samples, "_", simplify=TRUE)[,2]
virus[virus == "Mock"] <- "mock"

targets <- data.frame(
  sample = samples,
  host   = factor(host),
  virus  = factor(virus)
)

# ✅ ABSOLUTE GUARANTEE of alignment
stopifnot(all(targets$sample == colnames(E)))

# ===============================
# 5. Handle missing values
# ===============================
E[is.nan(E)] <- NA

# ===============================
# 6. Log transform (if needed)
# ===============================
if(max(E, na.rm=TRUE) > 50) {
  E <- log2(E + 1)
}

# ===============================
# 7. Quantile normalisation
# ===============================
E_norm <- normalizeBetweenArrays(E, method = "quantile")

# ===============================
# 8. QC plots (ggplot2, faceted)
# ===============================

plot_box <- function(mat, title) {
  
  df_long <- melt(mat)
  colnames(df_long) <- c("Protein","Sample","Expression")
  
  df_long <- merge(df_long, targets,
                   by.x="Sample", by.y="sample",
                   all.x=TRUE, sort=FALSE)
  
  ggplot(df_long, aes(x = Sample, y = Expression, fill = host)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.size=0.2) +
    facet_wrap(~virus, scales="free_x") +
    theme_minimal(base_size=11) +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_blank(),
      axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
      strip.text = element_text(size=8)
    ) +
    labs(title=title)
}

pdf("QC_before.pdf", width=14, height=8)
print(plot_box(E, "Before normalisation"))
dev.off()
pdf("QC_after.pdf", width=14, height=8)
print(plot_box(E_norm, "After quantile normalisation"))
dev.off()

# ===============================
# 9. PCA preparation
# ===============================

# Filter proteins (too many NAs)
keep <- rowSums(is.na(E_norm)) < (0.5 * ncol(E_norm))
E_pca <- E_norm[keep, ]

# Row-mean imputation (PCA only)
row_means <- rowMeans(E_pca, na.rm=TRUE)
for(i in 1:nrow(E_pca)){
  E_pca[i, is.na(E_pca[i,])] <- row_means[i]
}

# PCA
pca_result <- prcomp(t(E_pca), scale.=TRUE)

# Build PCA dataframe
pca_df <- as.data.frame(pca_result$x)
pca_df$sample <- rownames(pca_df)

# Merge metadata safely
pca_df <- merge(pca_df, targets, by="sample", sort=FALSE)

# ===============================
# 10. Variance explained plots
# ===============================
var_explained <- pca_result$sdev^2 / sum(pca_result$sdev^2)
cumvar <- cumsum(var_explained)

pdf("PCA_variance.pdf", width=10, height=6)
barplot(var_explained[1:10]*100,
        names.arg=paste0("PC",1:10),
        col="#0072B2",
        main="Variance per PC",
        ylab="% variance explained",
        las=2)
dev.off()
plot(cumvar[1:20]*100,
     type="b", pch=16,
     col="#D55E00",
     main="Cumulative variance",
     xlab="# PCs",
     ylab="Cumulative %",
     ylim=c(0,100))
abline(h=80, lty=2, col="grey50")

# dev.off()

cat("PC1:", round(var_explained[1]*100,1), "%\n")
cat("PC2:", round(var_explained[2]*100,1), "%\n")
cat("PC1+PC2:", round(sum(var_explained[1:2])*100,1), "%\n")

# ===============================
# 11. PCA plots (INDIVIDUAL, no function)
# ===============================
pdf("PC1_PC2.pdf", width=8, height=7)
ggplot(pca_df, aes(PC1, PC2, colour=host, shape=virus)) +
  geom_point(size=3, alpha=0.9) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = 1:length(unique(pca_df$virus))) +
  stat_ellipse(aes(group=host), level=0.95) +
  theme_minimal(base_size=13) +
  labs(
    title="PCA: PC1 vs PC2",
    x=paste0("PC1 (", round(var_explained[1]*100,1),"%)"),
    y=paste0("PC2 (", round(var_explained[2]*100,1),"%)")
  ) -> p1
print(p1)
dev.off()
# PC3 vs PC4
pdf("PC3_PC3.pdf", width=8, height=7)
ggplot(pca_df, aes(PC3, PC4, colour=host, shape=virus)) +
  geom_point(size=3, alpha=0.9) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = 1:length(unique(pca_df$virus))) +
  stat_ellipse(aes(group=host), level=0.95) +
  theme_minimal(base_size=13) +
  labs(
    title="PCA: PC3 vs PC4",
    x=paste0("PC3 (", round(var_explained[3]*100,1),"%)"),
    y=paste0("PC4 (", round(var_explained[4]*100,1),"%)")
  ) -> p2
print(p2)
dev.off()
# PC5 vs PC6
pdf("PC5_PC6.pdf", width=8, height=7)
ggplot(pca_df, aes(PC5, PC6, colour=host, shape=virus)) +
  geom_point(size=3, alpha=0.9) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = 1:length(unique(pca_df$virus))) +
  stat_ellipse(aes(group=host), level=0.95) +
  theme_minimal(base_size=13) +
  labs(    x=paste0("PC5 (", round(var_explained[5]*100,1),"%)"),
    y=paste0("PC6 (", round(var_explained[6]*100,1),"%)")) -> p3
print(p3)
dev.off()

#  library(uwot)        # UMAP

X <- t(E_norm)
for (j in 1:ncol(X)) {
  X[is.na(X[, j]), j] <- mean(X[, j], na.rm = TRUE)}
meta <- targets[match(rownames(X), targets$sample), ]
cols <- c("human"="red", "pig"="blue", "cow"="darkgreen")
n <- nrow(X)
neighbor_values <- c(2, 4, 6, 8)
neighbor_values <- neighbor_values[neighbor_values >= 2 & neighbor_values < n]
if (length(neighbor_values) == 0) { neighbor_values <- max(2, n - 1)}

# Run UMAP
pdf("UMAP_plots.pdf", width=10, height=8)
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
for (nn in neighbor_values) {
  set.seed(42)
  umap_result <- umap(  X,   n_neighbors = nn,
    min_dist = 0.1,  n_components = 2,  verbose = F  )
  coords <- umap_result
  plot(  coords[,1],  coords[,2], col = cols[meta$host],
    pch = 16, main = paste("n_neighbors =", nn),
    xlab = "UMAP 1",   ylab = "UMAP 2",  cex = 0.9 )
  legend("topright", legend = names(cols),
         col = cols,pch = 16,  cex = 0.8)}
dev.off()

par(mfrow = c(1,1))

# ===============================
# do pca for human only
E_human <- E_norm[, targets$host == "human"]
targets_human <- targets[targets$host == "human", ]
# Filter proteins (too many NAs)
keep_human <- rowSums(is.na(E_human)) < (0.5 * ncol(E_human))
E_human_pca <- E_human[keep_human, ]
# Row-mean imputation (PCA only)
row_means_human <- rowMeans(E_human_pca, na.rm=T)
for(i in 1:nrow(E_human_pca)){
  E_human_pca[i, is.na(E_human_pca[i,])] <- row_means_human[i] }
vars <- apply(E_human_pca, 1, var, na.rm=T)
E_human_pca <- E_human_pca[vars > 0, ]
pca_human <- prcomp(t(E_human_pca), scale.=T, center=T)
var_explained_h <- pca_human$sdev^2 / sum(pca_human$sdev^2)
pca_human_df <- as.data.frame(pca_human$x)
pca_human_df$sample <- rownames(pca_human_df)
pca_human_df <- merge(pca_human_df, targets_human,
                      by.x="sample", by.y="sample", sort=FALSE)
pdf("PCA_human_1_2.pdf", width=8, height=7)
h12 <- ggplot(pca_human_df, aes(PC1, PC2, colour=virus)) +
  geom_point(size=4, alpha=0.5) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = 1:length(unique(pca_human_df$virus))) +
  theme_minimal(base_size=13) +
  labs(  title="Human",
    x=paste0("PC1 (", round(var_explained_h[1]*100,1),"%)"),
    y=paste0("PC2 (", round(var_explained_h[2]*100,1),"%)") ) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(aes(label=gsub("A549_","",sample)), 
                           size=4, max.overlaps=990) 
dev.off()

pdf("PCA_human_3_4.pdf", width=8, height=7)
h34 <- ggplot(pca_human_df, aes(PC3, PC4, colour=virus)) +
  geom_point(size=4, alpha=0.5) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = 1:length(unique(pca_human_df$virus))) +
  theme_minimal(base_size=13) +
  labs(  title="Human",
    x=paste0("PC3 (", round(var_explained_h[3]*100,1),"%)"),
    y=paste0("PC4 (", round(var_explained_h[4]*100,1),"%)") ) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(aes(label=gsub("A549_","",sample)), 
                           size=4, max.overlaps=990)
dev.off()

## pig

E_pig <- E_norm[, targets$host == "pig"]
targets_pig <- targets[targets$host == "pig", ]
keep_pig <- rowSums(is.na(E_pig)) < (0.5 * ncol(E_pig))
E_pig_pca <- E_pig[keep_pig, ]

row_means_pig <- rowMeans(E_pig_pca, na.rm=TRUE)
for(i in 1:nrow(E_pig_pca)){
  E_pig_pca[i, is.na(E_pig_pca[i,])] <- row_means_pig[i]
}

vars <- apply(E_pig_pca, 1, var, na.rm=TRUE)
E_pig_pca <- E_pig_pca[vars > 0, ]

pca_pig <- prcomp(t(E_pig_pca), scale.=TRUE, center=TRUE)
var_explained_pig <- pca_pig$sdev^2 / sum(pca_pig$sdev^2)

pca_pig_df <- as.data.frame(pca_pig$x)
pca_pig_df$sample <- rownames(pca_pig_df)
pca_pig_df <- merge(pca_pig_df, targets_pig, by="sample", sort=FALSE)

pdf("PCA_pig_1_2.pdf", width=8, height=7)
p12 <- ggplot(pca_pig_df, aes(PC1, PC2, colour=virus, shape=virus)) +
  geom_point(size=4, alpha=0.6) +
  scale_shape_manual(values=1:length(unique(pca_pig_df$virus))) +
  theme_minimal(base_size=13) +
  labs( title="Pig",x=paste0("PC1 (",round(var_explained_pig[1]*100,1),"%)"),
    y=paste0("PC2 (", round(var_explained_pig[2]*100,1),"%)")) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(  aes(label=gsub("PK-15_","",sample)),
    size=4, max.overlaps=999  )
dev.off() 

pdf("PCA_pig_3_4.pdf", width=8, height=7)
p34 <- ggplot(pca_pig_df, aes(PC3, PC4, colour=virus, shape=virus)) +
  geom_point(size=4, alpha=0.6) +
  scale_shape_manual(values=1:length(unique(pca_pig_df$virus))) +
  theme_minimal(base_size=13) +
  labs( title="Pig",
    x=paste0("PC3 (", round(var_explained_pig[3]*100,1),"%)"),
    y=paste0("PC4 (", round(var_explained_pig[4]*100,1),"%)") ) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel( aes(label=gsub("PK-15_","",sample)),
    size=4, max.overlaps=999  )
dev.off()

## == COW PCA =======================

E_cow <- E_norm[, targets$host == "cow"]
targets_cow <- targets[targets$host == "cow", ]
keep_cow <- rowSums(is.na(E_cow)) < (0.5 * ncol(E_cow))
E_cow_pca <- E_cow[keep_cow, ]
row_means_cow <- rowMeans(E_cow_pca, na.rm=TRUE)
for(i in 1:nrow(E_cow_pca)){
  E_cow_pca[i, is.na(E_cow_pca[i,])] <- row_means_cow[i] }
vars <- apply(E_cow_pca, 1, var, na.rm=TRUE)
E_cow_pca <- E_cow_pca[vars > 0, ]
pca_cow <- prcomp(t(E_cow_pca), scale.=TRUE, center=TRUE)
var_explained_cow <- pca_cow$sdev^2 / sum(pca_cow$sdev^2)

pca_cow_df <- as.data.frame(pca_cow$x)
pca_cow_df$sample <- rownames(pca_cow_df)
pca_cow_df <- merge(pca_cow_df, targets_cow, by="sample", sort=FALSE)

pdf("PCA_cow_1_2.pdf", width=8, height=7) 
c12 <- ggplot(pca_cow_df, aes(PC1, PC2, colour=virus, shape=virus)) +
  geom_point(size=4, alpha=0.6) +
  scale_shape_manual(values=1:length(unique(pca_cow_df$virus))) +
  theme_minimal(base_size=13) +
  labs(    title="Cow",
    x=paste0("PC1 (", round(var_explained_cow[1]*100,1),"%)"),
    y=paste0("PC2 (", round(var_explained_cow[2]*100,1),"%)")) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(  aes(label=gsub("MDBK_","",sample)),
    size=4, max.overlaps=999  )
dev.off()

pdf("PCA_cow_3_4.pdf", width=8, height=7) 
c34 <- ggplot(pca_cow_df, aes(PC3, PC4, colour=virus, shape=virus)) +
  geom_point(size=4, alpha=0.6) +
  scale_shape_manual(values=1:length(unique(pca_cow_df$virus))) +
  theme_minimal(base_size=13) +
  labs(    title="Cow",
    x=paste0("PC3 (", round(var_explained_cow[3]*100,1),"%)"),
    y=paste0("PC4 (", round(var_explained_cow[4]*100,1),"%)")) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(  aes(label=gsub("MDBK_","",sample)),
    size=4, max.overlaps=999)
dev.off()

library(gridExtra)
pdf("PCA_all.pdf", width=15, height=15)
grid.arrange(  h12, h34,
  p12, p34,  c12, c34,
  ncol = 2, nrow =3 )
dev.off()

#  genes

# ===============================
# HUMAN loadings → gene lists
# ===============================
num=100
loadings_human <- as.data.frame(pca_human$rotation[, 1:2])
loadings_human$Gene <- rownames(loadings_human)
top_pc1_human <- loadings_human[
  order(abs(loadings_human$PC1), decreasing=TRUE), ][1:num, c("Gene","PC1")]
top_pc2_human <- loadings_human[
  order(abs(loadings_human$PC2), decreasing=TRUE), ][1:num, c("Gene","PC2")]

loadings_pig <- as.data.frame(pca_pig$rotation[, 1:2])
loadings_pig$Gene <- rownames(loadings_pig)
top_pc1_pig <- loadings_pig[
  order(abs(loadings_pig$PC1), decreasing=TRUE), ][1:num, c("Gene","PC1")]
top_pc2_pig <- loadings_pig[
  order(abs(loadings_pig$PC2), decreasing=TRUE), ][1:num, c("Gene","PC2")]

loadings_cow <- as.data.frame(pca_cow$rotation[, 1:2])
loadings_cow$Gene <- rownames(loadings_cow)
top_pc1_cow <- loadings_cow[
  order(abs(loadings_cow$PC1), decreasing=TRUE), ][1:num, c("Gene","PC1")]
top_pc2_cow <- loadings_cow[
  order(abs(loadings_cow$PC2), decreasing=TRUE), ][1:num, c("Gene","PC2")]

# intersection
sort(intersect(top_pc1_human$Gene, top_pc1_pig$Gene)) # 14
sort(intersect(top_pc1_human$Gene, top_pc1_cow$Gene) )# 3
sort(intersect(top_pc1_pig$Gene, top_pc1_cow$Gene)  ) # 18

intersect(intersect(top_pc1_human$Gene, top_pc1_pig$Gene), top_pc1_cow$Gene)
# none shared across all 3 hosts in PC1 top num
